## 1. Executive Summary

Universe Simulation is a multiplayer 3D browser survival game set inside a procedurally generated planet. Players sign in through a web browser, spawn into a shared open world, and survive using the same physical and chemical laws that govern the simulation itself. Fire is not a scripted visual effect — it is a genuine combustion reaction running in a 3D grid of simulation cells using the Arrhenius equation from chemistry. Metal smelting is not a progress bar — it is a real chemical reduction of copper or iron ore at the correct temperature. The world has day and night, four seasons, rain, snow, and storms, all driven by real mathematical models.

As of this report (version 27, 2026-03-26), the game has completed milestones M0 through M15. The full progression arc spans the Stone Age, Copper Age, Iron Age, Gunpowder Era, Industrial Age, Space Age, and now First Contact with an alien civilization. A player can start with nothing and — over many sessions — gather stone, craft tools, light a real fire, cook food, treat wounds with herbs, smelt copper and iron using real chemistry, build and defend settlements with castle walls, fire a musket using real gunpowder chemistry, launch a rocket into space, decode an alien signal using Morse code, send a probe to an orbiting planet, operate a nuclear reactor, travel to another planet via interplanetary transit, and interact with a full alien language and gateway system. The companion website at https://universe-companion.vercel.app explains the science behind every mechanic in plain language and shows a live Universe Map of all discovered worlds.

The most recent milestones (M14 and M15) added interplanetary transit, the Velar alien language puzzle, a gateway system for traveling between universes, and a public Universe Map on the companion site. Twelve playtesting sessions (Cycles 1 through 12) were completed on 2026-03-24 and 2026-03-25, finding and fixing six bugs. The game is stable, production-ready in its core survival loop, and actively expanding its late-game content.

A note on sprint numbering: the user's request refers to "sprints M57-M63." This report uses the project's own milestone numbering (M0 through M15). Section 4 covers every milestone in order. If a separate sprint numbering system exists outside this codebase, it was not found in any file reviewed.

---

## 2. Application Overview

**What does this app do?**

It is a multiplayer 3D open-world survival game played entirely inside a web browser. Players explore a shared planet, manage five survival stats (health, hunger, thirst, energy, and stamina), gather resources, craft items, smelt metals using real chemistry, build shelters, interact with NPC settlements, trade with a live supply-and-demand economy, commit crimes, hunt animals, sail waterways, fish, conduct diplomacy, fire muskets and rockets, decode alien signals, and travel to other planets. The world clock runs continuously even when no players are online.

**Who is it for?**

Players interested in open-world survival and crafting games, particularly those who enjoy understanding how the underlying systems actually work. The game's science grounding — real combustion chemistry, real reduction reactions, real orbital mechanics, real Arrhenius kinetics — makes it appealing to curious players who want their actions to feel grounded in reality rather than arbitrary game rules.

**What problem does it solve?**

It provides a persistent shared survival world that is accessible without installing any software. All 3D graphics, physics simulation, and chemistry calculations run inside the browser. The science grounding means players can learn real chemistry, metallurgy, and orbital mechanics through play. The companion site extends this by explaining the science in plain English.

**Technology stack (explained simply)**

| Technology | Plain-English Role |
|---|---|
| React | The framework that builds and updates the on-screen interface |
| Three.js / React Three Fiber | The 3D rendering engine that draws the world |
| Vite | Bundles the code so browsers can run it |
| TypeScript | Strict-typed JavaScript — catches mistakes before the game runs |
| Zustand | Stores and shares game state (vitals, position, inventory, etc.) |
| Clerk | Handles user login and session management |
| Neon Postgres | Cloud database for player saves, NPC memory, criminal records, discoveries |
| Vercel | Hosts the frontend website, the HTTP API endpoints, and the companion site |
| Railway | Hosts the always-on WebSocket server that manages the live world |
| WebSocket | Persistent two-way connection for real-time multiplayer |
| Rapier3D | WebAssembly physics engine for character movement, collisions, and river current push |
| bitecs | Entity-component system managing player and creature data |
| Web Workers | Background threads running chemistry, thermal, fluid, and physics calculations |
| SharedArrayBuffer | Shared memory between workers and main thread for the simulation grid |

## 4. All Major Systems — Milestone by Milestone

### M0: Universe Visible

The first milestone made the world feel alive before any player interaction. Six wood clusters are pre-ignited at world start so fire is visible immediately. A 20-minute real-time day/night cycle rotates the sun around the planet. Biome temperatures initialize correctly in the simulation grid — mountain peaks at -5 degrees C, desert lowlands at 35 degrees C, sea level at 15 degrees C. Ten organisms spawn from the genome encoder and wander using simple AI. The SimGrid Visualizer (Tab key) overlays a temperature heat map on the terrain.

**Key files**: DayNightCycle.tsx, FireRenderer.tsx, SimGridVisualizer.tsx, CreatureRenderer.tsx

---

### M1 through M4: The Seven Survival Slices

Seven end-to-end gameplay loops verified and working:

1. **Gather** — Press F near any resource node to collect it. Nodes include stone, flint, wood, clay, fiber, copper ore, iron ore, coal, sand, sulfur, bark, and herbs. Nodes respawn after 60 seconds.
2. **Craft and Equip** — Open the Crafting panel (C key), select a recipe, craft. Items appear in inventory and can be equipped from there.
3. **Light Fire** — Gather tinder and wood. Equip flint. Press the attack key near wood. The game calls real Arrhenius combustion chemistry. Fire ignites and burns genuinely.
4. **Cook and Eat** — Place raw food near a hot fire cell (above 200 degrees C). The thermal system converts it to cooked food. Eating restores the hunger vital.
5. **Treat Wounds** — Taking damage creates a wound with a bacterial load. The logistic growth equation governs bacterial growth. Applying gathered herbs reduces the load until the wound heals.
6. **Build Shelter and Sleep** — Craft a bedroll (3 Fiber + 2 Wood). Place it. Press Z to sleep. Stamina restores. Bedroll becomes the respawn anchor.
7. **Smelt Copper** — Build a basic furnace from stone and clay. Light fire beneath it. Real chemical reduction: Cu2S + C + heat produces copper metal and SO2 gas. Craft copper tools.

---

### M5: Shared World

The game became genuinely multiplayer in this milestone:
- Resource depletion is server-authoritative: when one player destroys a node, all players see it gone
- Fire ignition broadcasts over WebSocket — other players see fires you start
- New players joining receive a snapshot of the current world state
- Player nameplates (Clerk usernames) float above each player's head
- The death system drops inventory as pickable loot and respawns at the placed bedroll
- Criminal record (murder_count) persists in the Neon database through death and reconnection
- Photorealism pass added: wet terrain edges, rock specular highlights, subsurface scattering on creatures, post-processing bloom and vignette

---

### M6: NPC Civilization

Five named settlements appeared in the world: Ashford (plains), Ironhaven (mid-elevation near iron), Saltmere (coastal), Thornwall (forested highlands), and Ridgepost (rocky ridge). Each settlement:
- Advances its civilization level by crafting and researching using the same chemistry rules as players
- Has NPC memory stored in Neon DB — trust and threat scores per player, persisting across sessions
- Controls a 150-meter territory radius that NPCs patrol and defend
- Has a trade system: TRADE_OFFER and TRADE_ACCEPT messages transfer inventory items
- Closes its gate when a player's threat score exceeds 3
- Shows a SettlementHUD with reaction banners (Friendly / Wary / Hostile)

---

### M7: Iron Age and PvP Outlaw

Iron ore appears on mid-elevation terrain following sedimentary placement rules. The full iron chain requires a blast furnace (8 stone + 4 clay) that reaches over 1,000 degrees C. Real chemistry: Fe2O3 + 3C reduces to iron metal and CO2. Iron tools and weapons were added.

The outlaw system tracks kills in the Neon database. NPC reactions escalate with murder count: wariness, gate closure at 3 kills, and an active WANTED bounty label above the player's head at 5 kills. Redemption quests (escort, delivery, defense) reduce the murder count. Criminal records survive death — "Your crimes are remembered" appears on the death screen.

---

### M8: Weather

The Markov-chain weather system runs continuously:
- States: CLEAR, CLOUDY, RAIN, STORM, SNOW
- Rain: 2,000 instanced falling line segments
- Snow: 800 instanced billboard quads with spiral descent (below 0 degrees C)
- Storm: random lightning flashes every 15-45 seconds
- Wind: 300 dust and leaf sprites in clear and cloudy states

Fire extinguishing during rain is built but not yet wired to the chemistry grid (M8 Track 1 is in progress). Steel age (carburization and quenching) remains planned for M8 Track 2.

---

### M9: Rivers, Animals, and Performance

Three parallel tracks completed in one milestone:

**Rivers (Track 1)**
RiverSystem.ts uses a flow-field erosion algorithm to carve ten rivers deterministically from the world seed. The resulting valley geometry is real 3D terrain modification — visible depressions in the planet surface. RiverRenderer.tsx draws each river as a ribbon mesh with a Fresnel water shader that mimics real water surface optics. Players wading in rivers are physically pushed downstream by the Rapier character controller. Pressing F near a river restores the hydration vital. Clay nodes spawn on riverbanks. A droplet icon appears in the HUD when the player is within drinking range.

**Animals (Track 2)**
Three species with distinct behavior:
- Deer: flock together (separation/cohesion/alignment rules), flee from players and wolves
- Wolves: travel in packs, coordinate hunts with driver/flanker roles, attack players in territory
- Boars: solitary or small groups, charge the player when threatened

AnimalRenderer uses Three.js InstancedMesh — one GPU draw call per species regardless of population. A three-tier LOD system adjusts detail by distance. Hunting yields raw_meat, hide, bone, wolf_pelt, and boar_tusk. New crafting: leather (from hide), leather armor, bone needle, tusk knife.

**Performance (Track 3)**
- Worker tick rates standardized: fluid/chemistry at 20 Hz, thermal at 10 Hz
- WebSocket BATCH_UPDATE batches non-critical state deltas
- NodeHealthBars pool: 32 pre-allocated objects, reused to eliminate garbage collection pauses
- Terrain shader warmed up at startup via gl.compile() to prevent visible stutter on first render

---

### M10: Seasons, Sailing, and Trade Economy

**Seasons (Track A)**
SeasonSystem.js runs a 40-real-time-minute year divided into four equal 10-minute seasons. SeasonalTerrainPass.tsx applies visual overlays: amber tint in autumn, snow layer in winter, blossom particles in spring, baseline in summer. Winter applies a +20% hunger drain rate penalty. The SeasonWidget in the HUD shows the current season name and day-of-year.

**Sailing and Fishing (Track B)**
Two craftable watercraft: a wind-driven raft (downwind only) and a sailing boat with real tacking physics and a 45-degree no-go zone directly into the wind. SailingRenderer.tsx draws both vessels as procedural meshes. A fishing rod (cast and wait for a bite, then react within a window) produces fish that can be cooked and eaten. A compass item displays magnetic bearing in the HUD.

**Trade Economy (Track C)**
TradeEconomy.js manages settlement_stockpiles in Neon DB. Supply and demand drive prices between 0.6x and 2.5x base price. Each settlement specializes: Ashford (wood/leather), Ironhaven (metals), Saltmere (fish/salt), Thornwall (stone/ore), Ridgepost (food/grain). NPC caravans walk between settlements every 5 real-time minutes, rebalancing stockpiles. ShopHUD provides buy and sell tabs with live prices. Copper coins are the currency.

---

### M11: Gunpowder, Castles, Astronomy, and Diplomacy

**Gunpowder**
KNO3 + C + S now runs as a real Arrhenius combustion reaction. Saltpeter forms in nitrogen-rich soil near animal waste and cave floors. Sulfur appears in volcanic terrain zones. The musket (iron barrel + powder charge) is the first chemical-propulsion firearm.

**Castles**
Three new construction blocks: wall segment, tower (corner and inline), and gate. CastleRenderer handles all three. Siege mechanics allow attackers to damage walls and batter down gates. City walls unlock for NPC settlements at civLevel 4, player castles at civLevel 5.

**Astronomy**
The star field was upgraded with real IAU constellation position data. A craftable telescope (4 iron ingot + 2 glass ingot) reveals constellation labels in a HUD overlay when equipped at night. Moon phase is tracked against the world clock. Eclipse timing is computed from orbital mechanics.

**Diplomacy**
Each settlement promotes its highest-trust NPC as mayor. Diplomatic envoys walk visibly between settlements to open relations. Settlements can declare war or peace. The player's criminal alignment shifts based on which side they support.

---

### M12: Space Age — Rockets, Radio, and Electric Light

**Rocket System**
RocketSystem.ts runs a three-phase state machine: countdown (T-10 seconds), ignition, and ascent. On ascent, ROCKET_LAUNCHED broadcasts to all players with a screen shake. RocketVFXRenderer.tsx produces a three-layer exhaust cone (white-hot inner core, orange-amber middle, dark soot outer dispersal, 180 particles) with a ShaderMaterial heat shimmer above the plume and a scorch disc on the terrain at launch.

**Radio**
RadioSystem.ts maintains a broadcast queue and tower registry. Activating a tower sends PLAYER_RADIO_BROADCAST to all clients. RadioTowerVFXRenderer.tsx draws teal electromagnetic pulse rings using additive blending at one ring per two seconds.

**Electric Lighting**
At civLevel 6, ElectricLightPass.tsx activates 40 Three.js PointLights above settlement buildings — warm 2700K tungsten white (#FFBA74), night-only, with a natural filament flicker. This makes civLevel 6 settlements visible from across the planet at night.

**civLevel 6 Gate and Velar Teaser**
CIVILIZATION_L6 broadcasts when a settlement reaches civLevel 6, unlocking electronics, rocketry, aerospace, and nuclear physics tech branches. VelarSignalView.tsx shows a Morse-blink signal panel 30 seconds after the first rocket launch — a narrative hook for first contact.

---

### M13: First Contact, Orbital Mechanics, and Nuclear Reactor

**Velar Signal and First Contact (Track A)**
DecoderPanel.tsx accepts 8-symbol Morse code input (DOT and DASH buttons). Entering the correct Velar cipher sequence broadcasts VELAR_DECODED to all connected players. FirstContactOverlay.tsx plays a 12-second cinematic — 200 animated stars with a scintillation shader — and writes the event to two new Neon DB tables (discoveries and planets). The companion site gains a FirstContactBanner that polls every 15 seconds and displays a full-width announcement when first contact is confirmed.

**Orbital Mechanics (Track B)**
OrbitalMechanicsSystem.ts simulates three planets in real Kepler elliptical orbits: Sulfis at 0.4 AU (innermost, mineral-rich), Aethon at 0.7 AU (temperate), and Velar at 2.1 AU (outermost, source of the signal). OrbitalView.tsx renders a 400x400 SVG solar map with live planetary positions and a capsule launch arc. The Orbital Capsule (ITEM 66, recipe 100: 5x circuit_board + 10x steel_ingot, 1200s craft time) can be launched at any planet; on arrival the server broadcasts PROBE_LANDED with seeded surface data (terrain type, mineral composition, atmospheric conditions).

**Nuclear Reactor (Track C)**
NuclearReactorSystem.ts models a 100 kW reactor: fission raises temperature at +40 degrees C per second, water cooling lowers it at -60 degrees C per second, and exceeding 800 degrees C triggers meltdown, creating a 20-metre radiation zone dealing 2 HP per second. A cleanup mission (10 clay + 5 stone) removes the zone. ReactorWidget shows a live temperature bar in the HUD — green, amber, or red. New manufacturing stations: electric_forge (smelts three items simultaneously) and arc_welder (required for advanced steel assembly). MAT.HYDROGEN (ID 68) is produced as a byproduct of water electrolysis at the reactor.

---

### M14: Interplanetary Transit, Velar Language, and Universe Registry

**Interplanetary Transit (Track A)**
InterplanetaryTransitSystem.ts triggers when the player presses F at a launch pad with an Orbital Capsule in inventory. A 20-second transit begins: TransitOverlay.tsx plays a full-screen canvas cinematic with 300 stars streaming outward with motion blur, the destination planet growing from a point to a sphere, a velocity counter, and a distance countdown. On arrival, DestinationPlanet.tsx generates a unique procedural cube-sphere planet from the destination seed — new FBM terrain, biome-colored vertex colors, ocean, atmosphere, and 50 resource node dots on land.

**Velar Language System (Track B)**
VelarLanguageSystem.ts generates a procedural 5-glyph SVG alien alphabet seeded from the world seed. Five concepts are encoded: life, star, path, here, come — each with a unique geometric grammar (spiral helix, radial spokes, flowing curve, concentric rings, open arc). VelarResponsePanel.tsx presents the 5 glyphs as a decoding puzzle: the player matches each glyph to its concept using hint tooltips. On completion, VELAR_RESPONSE_DECODED broadcasts. VelarGatewayRenderer.tsx draws a 6-metre torus ring with teal emissive glow, 80 orbiting halo particles, and a rotating inner energy disc when active. ITEM 67 (Velar Key) and MAT 69 (Velar Crystal) were added as new craftable items.

**Universe Registry and Multiverse Infrastructure (Track C)**
UniverseRegistry.js on the server manages a `universes` Neon DB table tracking seed, name, origin, player count, tech level, and discovery count. When the Velar Gateway is activated, the server spawns a new Velar World universe entry. The in-process room registry handles multi-universe routing so players in different universes receive messages only from their own world.

**Bug fixes in M14**
- VelarLanguageSystem.ts: invalid hex literal `0xve1a4a1f` corrected to `0xe1a4a1f`
- Inventory.ts: duplicate `itemId` key in recipe 104 output object

**New IDs established**: MAT 69 (Velar Crystal), ITEM 67 (Velar Key), Recipes 104-105. Next available: MAT 70, ITEM 68, Recipe 106.

---

### M15: Universe Map on Companion Site

A new page at https://universe-companion.vercel.app/universes shows a live map of all discovered universes:
- SVG starfield canvas with universe nodes rendered as colored spheres
- Gateway connection lines (teal dashes for the Velar Gateway origin, dim lines for others)
- A detail panel per universe showing civilization progress bar, tech level label, discovery count, active players, and founding date
- A universe list sidebar
- Polls every 30 seconds for updates
- A new API endpoint (app/api/universes/route.ts) queries the Neon DB universes table with a 60-second in-process cache and falls back to a home universe stub if the database is unavailable
- The companion site header now includes a "UNIVERSE MAP" navigation link

## 6. User Interface and Design

**Visual Theme**
The UI uses a consistent visual language: dark near-black backgrounds, semi-transparent frosted panels with blur effect, white monospace typography, rust-orange and blue accent colors. The HUD uses a Rust-game-style design with icon plus horizontal fill bars for each vital. Warning states glow red.

**HUD Layout**
- Top-left: Vitals panel (always visible during play) — five vital bars with icons, hotbar below, ambient temperature display, RiverHUD droplet indicator when near freshwater, SeasonWidget showing current season and day of year, astronomy overlay when telescope is equipped at night, ReactorWidget temperature bar when near a nuclear reactor
- Top-right: Clock and connection status
- Right edge: 48px icon strip with sidebar panel buttons
- Full viewport: 3D canvas behind the UI
- Center: Crosshair when pointer is locked; click-to-play overlay when not
- Bottom-right: Admin button (admin accounts only)
- On-screen HUD elements: SettlementHUD reaction banners, WANTED labels above outlaw players, player nameplates

**HUD Vitals — Five Bars**

| Vital | Icon | What It Measures |
|---|---|---|
| Health | Red heart | Drops on damage, starvation, dehydration, cold |
| Satiety | Food icon | Hunger — empties in approximately 20 minutes |
| Hydration | Water drop | Thirst — empties in approximately 12 minutes |
| Energy | Lightning | Drains with exertion |
| Stamina | Sleep icon | Depletes over time, restored by sleeping |

**Ambient Temperature Display**
Below the vitals: "AMBIENT: XX.X C" with color coding — blue below 0 degrees C, green from 0 to 30 degrees C, orange from 30 to 50 degrees C, red above 50 degrees C.

**Panel Animation**
Panels slide in from the right over 250 milliseconds. When any panel opens, the mouse cursor is released and game input is paused.

**Responsiveness**
The game requires pointer lock (mouse-look mode), WASD keyboard input, and a wide layout. Not usable on mobile or touch devices.

---

## 7. Performance Analysis

**Rendering**
- Three.js canvas fills the entire viewport with antialiasing and high-performance GPU preference
- Shadow maps at 2048x2048 — high quality, GPU-intensive
- Post-processing: Bloom and Vignette passes active
- 5,000 stars via Stars component
- AnimalRenderer: InstancedMesh — one draw call per species regardless of population count
- Creature LOD: three tiers selected automatically by camera distance
- Terrain shader warmed up via gl.compile() at startup

**Simulation Workers**
- Chemistry worker: 10 Hz in its own thread — does not block rendering
- Thermal worker: 10 Hz for per-material heat diffusion
- Fluid/flow worker: 20 Hz for river flow-field simulation
- Physics worker: 60 Hz Rapier3D physics

**Network**
- Player position: 10 updates per second per client
- Server to client: 10 Hz WORLD_SNAPSHOT
- Non-critical world state batched into BATCH_UPDATE messages
- Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff

**Known Performance Concerns**
- Shadow camera frustum covers the full planet — may be slow on integrated graphics or older hardware
- Large simultaneous fires: each burning cell adds one draw call and one point light
- ElectricLightPass: 40 PointLights per civLevel 6 settlement, night-only — PointLights are among Three.js's most GPU-expensive light types; not yet benchmarked with multiple civLevel 6 settlements active simultaneously
- RocketVFXRenderer: 180 particles active during ascent — one-time per launch event, not per frame at rest
- OrbitalMechanicsSystem: Kepler calculations for 3 planets each tick — CPU cost negligible; OrbitalView SVG re-renders on each position update
- NuclearReactorSystem: temperature simulation per reactor per tick — scales with active reactor count
- TransitOverlay: 300 streaming star particles during 20-second transit — one-time cinematic cost

---

## 8. Accessibility Review

The application has limited accessibility support appropriate for a mouse-and-keyboard 3D survival game.

**What works**
- Sidebar icon buttons include aria-label attributes
- Icon buttons use aria-pressed to indicate active state
- Close buttons on panels have aria-label="Close panel"
- Time control buttons include aria-label and aria-pressed
- Vital bars use percentage text labels alongside color
- Temperature display uses both color and a numeric value

**What is missing or problematic**
- The game requires pointer lock — no keyboard-only play mode
- No focus management when panels open
- No focus trap inside panels
- Secondary text color contrast may fail WCAG AA requirements
- No reduced-motion support

**WCAG Compliance**: Approximately Level A for labeled UI elements. The 3D gameplay is inaccessible by design given the medium.

---

## 9. Security Assessment

**Authentication**
Clerk handles all authentication. Vercel API routes verify the Clerk JWT on every request using CLERK_SECRET_KEY. This is a correct and secure pattern.

**Authorization**
- /api/save and /api/load verify that the requesting user matches the data being accessed
- /api/world-settings POST and /api/admin verify the admin user ID server-side
- The admin button is hidden client-side using VITE_ADMIN_USER_ID — this is UI convenience only; real security is on the server

**Identified Risks**

| Risk | Severity | Explanation |
|---|---|---|
| ADMIN_SET_TIME WebSocket command has no authentication | Medium | Any client connected to the Railway WebSocket can send ADMIN_SET_TIME and change the world clock for all players. The server does not verify admin identity. |
| admin.ts still uses Web API style (Request/Response) | Medium | This endpoint was not converted during the API fix session. It returns 500 errors in Vercel's Node.js runtime. The admin player list fails to load. |
| Status site connects to WebSocket with no authentication | Low | The status page joins the server as "__status_viewer__". No sensitive data beyond position and health is exposed, but the feed is unauthenticated. |
| No rate limiting on save/load endpoints | Low | A malicious user could spam /api/save. Vercel's default limits provide some protection but no explicit rate limit is configured. |
| Velar cipher validation is client-side | Low | The correct 8-symbol sequence is checked in DecoderPanel.tsx. A player who inspects the JavaScript bundle could extract the answer and bypass the puzzle. |
| /api/init-db has no authentication | Low | Anyone who knows the URL can call it. Safe to call repeatedly (uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) but should be protected after setup. |

---

## 10. Code Quality

**Overall Assessment**: The code is well-organized and readable. The separation between frontend, API layer, and game server is clean. Simulation modules (src/sim/) are pure functions with no side effects. Systems added in M9 through M14 follow the same pattern as earlier systems — self-contained, domain-scoped, no cross-contamination between concerns.

**Strengths**
- TypeScript throughout, consistent strict mode
- Zustand stores are small and domain-scoped: player, game, multiplayer, UI, save, settlement, outlaw, weather, transit, velar
- WorldSocket class handles reconnection logic correctly with no memory leaks
- MaterialRegistry, GridCoords, Arrhenius are pure functions with zero side effects
- All simulation modules use real physical constants from constants.ts
- SurvivalSystems.ts covers all 7 survival slices cleanly
- DeathSystem correctly drops inventory as world pickups and respawns at bedroll anchor
- AnimalRenderer uses InstancedMesh — zero per-animal draw calls
- NodeHealthBars pool is pre-allocated (32 slots) — zero GC pressure during node interactions
- fluidWorker runs at 20 Hz on its own thread — does not block rendering
- Save operation uses SQL upsert (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE) — correct pattern
- Status site is fully isolated in src/status/ — no dependencies on game stores, auth, or 3D libraries
- removeItemForce() in Inventory.ts ensures building resource consumption works in God Mode (B-18, fixed 2026-03-25)
- canAddItem() validation in Inventory.craft() ensures materials are never consumed if the output cannot fit in inventory (B-19, fixed 2026-03-25)

**Weaknesses**
- admin.ts uses the old Web API Request/Response style — real production failure
- Settings panel remains an empty placeholder
- InventoryPanel uses a useState counter (forceRefresh) as a manual refresh trigger — not reactive state
- saveGame() does not notify the user on failure
- Test coverage exists only for the three sim modules (MaterialRegistry, GridCoords, Arrhenius) — the rest of the codebase is untested

**Testing**
- Vitest is configured
- 18 tests covering MaterialRegistry (4), GridCoords (8), and Arrhenius (6) — all passing
- No test coverage for survival systems, API routes, WebSocket behavior, NPC systems, settlement logic, RiverSystem, AnimalAISystem, or any M10 through M14 systems

## 12. APIs and Integrations

### Vercel API Routes (HTTP)

| Endpoint | Method | Auth Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| /api/save | POST | Yes | Upserts player state to player_saves |
| /api/load | GET | Yes | Retrieves player state from player_saves |
| /api/world-settings | GET | No | Returns time_scale from world_settings |
| /api/world-settings | POST | Yes (admin) | Updates time_scale in world_settings |
| /api/admin | GET | Yes (admin) | Returns all player records — currently broken (B-01) |
| /api/init-db | GET | No | Creates database tables — one-time setup |

### Railway WebSocket Server (Real-time)

**Client to Server messages**:

| Message Type | When Sent |
|---|---|
| JOIN | On connection |
| PLAYER_UPDATE | 10 Hz (game clients) |
| NODE_DESTROYED | When player gathers a node |
| FIRE_STARTED | When player lights fire |
| PLAYER_KILLED | When player kills another |
| TRADE_OFFER | Player initiates trade with NPC |
| TRADE_ACCEPT | NPC accepts trade |
| SHOP_BUY | Player buys from settlement shop |
| SHOP_SELL | Player sells to settlement shop |
| ADMIN_SET_TIME | Admin changes time scale |
| INTERPLANETARY_TRANSIT_LAUNCHED | Player begins transit with orbital capsule (M14) |
| TRANSIT_ARRIVED | Player arrives at destination planet (M14) |
| VELAR_RESPONSE | Player submits Velar language response (M14) |
| VELAR_GATEWAY_ACTIVATED | Player activates the Velar Gateway (M14) |

**Server to Client messages**:

| Message Type | When Sent |
|---|---|
| WORLD_SNAPSHOT | 10 Hz to all clients |
| BATCH_UPDATE | As needed — batched non-critical deltas |
| PLAYER_JOINED | New player connects |
| PLAYER_LEFT | Player disconnects |
| NODE_DESTROYED | Resource node depleted |
| FIRE_STARTED | Fire ignited |
| PLAYER_KILLED | Player death event |
| SETTLEMENT_UNLOCKED_IRON | Settlement reaches civLevel 2 |
| BOUNTY_COLLECTED | Bounty claimed |
| SEASON_CHANGED | Season transitions |
| STOCKPILE_UPDATE | Settlement stockpile changed |
| CARAVAN_SPAWNED | NPC caravan begins journey |
| DIPLOMACY_STATE_CHANGED | Settlement war/peace state changed (M11) |
| ROCKET_LAUNCHED | First rocket launch event (M12) |
| PLAYER_RADIO_BROADCAST | Player activates a radio tower (M12) |
| CIVILIZATION_L6 | Settlement reaches civLevel 6 (M12) |
| VELAR_DECODED | Player successfully decodes the Velar signal (M13) |
| PROBE_LANDED | Orbital capsule arrives at a planet (M13) |
| INTERPLANETARY_TRANSIT_LAUNCHED | Broadcast to all on transit start (M14) |
| TRANSIT_ARRIVED_BROADCAST | Broadcast to all on transit arrival (M14) |
| VELAR_GATEWAY_REVEALED | Gateway coordinates sent to all after language decoded (M14) |
| VELAR_GATEWAY_ACTIVATED | New universe spawned; universe list broadcast (M14) |
| VELAR_RESPONSE | Server sends Velar probe response 5 seconds after probe lands on Velar (M14) |

## 13. Data and Database

### Tables

**player_saves** — one row per player

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| user_id | TEXT (primary key) | Clerk user ID |
| username | TEXT | Display name |
| pos_x, pos_y, pos_z | REAL | Last known world position |
| health, hunger, thirst, energy, fatigue | REAL | Vitals (0 to 1) |
| ev_points | INTEGER | Accumulated evolution points |
| civ_tier | INTEGER | Current civilization tier |
| discoveries | TEXT | JSON array of discovery IDs |
| current_goal | TEXT | Active high-level goal |
| sim_seconds | REAL | Cumulative simulation time |
| murder_count | INTEGER | Criminal record — persists through death |
| updated_at | TIMESTAMPTZ | Last save timestamp |

**world_settings** — a single row

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| id | Integer | Always 1 |
| time_scale | REAL | Current simulation speed multiplier |
| sim_time | REAL | Authoritative simulation clock in seconds |
| updated_at | TIMESTAMPTZ | Last write timestamp |

**npc_memory** — one row per (NPC / player) pair

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| settlement_id | TEXT | Settlement identifier |
| player_id | TEXT | Clerk user ID |
| trust | REAL | Trust score (-1 to 1) |
| threat | INTEGER | Threat level (0-5+) |
| last_interaction | TIMESTAMPTZ | When the NPC last encountered this player |

**settlement_stockpiles** — one row per (settlement / material) pair (M10, requires manual creation)

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| settlement_id | TEXT | Settlement identifier |
| material_id | INTEGER | Material ID from the material registry |
| quantity | REAL | Current stockpile quantity |
| updated_at | TIMESTAMPTZ | Last write timestamp |

**discoveries** — one row per discovery event (M13, requires manual creation)

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| id | SERIAL (primary key) | Auto-increment row ID |
| discovery_type | TEXT | Category (e.g., "FIRST_CONTACT") |
| player_id | TEXT | Clerk user ID of the player who triggered the discovery |
| data | JSONB | Discovery-specific payload |
| created_at | TIMESTAMPTZ | When the discovery was recorded |

**planets** — one row per probe-surveyed planet (M13, requires manual creation)

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| id | SERIAL (primary key) | Auto-increment row ID |
| planet_name | TEXT | Planet name (Aethon, Velar, or Sulfis) |
| terrain_type | TEXT | Surface terrain descriptor |
| mineral_composition | JSONB | Map of mineral names to abundance percentages |
| atmospheric_conditions | JSONB | Pressure, temperature, gas composition |
| surveyed_at | TIMESTAMPTZ | When the probe arrived |
| surveyed_by | TEXT | Clerk user ID of the player who launched the capsule |

**universes** — one row per discovered universe (M14)

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| seed | TEXT | World generation seed (primary key) |
| name | TEXT | Universe display name |
| origin | TEXT | How this universe was discovered |
| player_count | INTEGER | Active players in this universe |
| tech_level | INTEGER | Highest civilization tier achieved |
| discovery_count | INTEGER | Number of discovery events logged |

### Data Concerns

- discoveries column in player_saves stored as a serialized JSON string in a TEXT column rather than native JSONB — cannot be indexed or queried directly
- No migration system — schema changes require manual SQL
- Simulation grid (fire, heat, river flow) is client-side only — not persisted; active fires are lost on disconnect
- settlement_stockpiles, discoveries, and planets tables require manual creation; /api/init-db does not create them automatically
- Nuclear meltdown radiation zone is client-side state only — lost on page refresh

### Next Available IDs

| Type | Next ID |
|---|---|
| Material (MAT) | 70 |
| Item (ITEM) | 68 |
| Recipe | 106 |

## 14. Deployment and Infrastructure

### Frontend (Vercel)

React application built with Vite, deployed to Vercel. Two named entry points in vite.config.ts:
- `main` — the game, at index.html
- `status` — the dashboard, at status/index.html

Both compile in a single build pass.

**Required browser headers** (vercel.json):
- Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin — required for SharedArrayBuffer
- Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp — required alongside the above

These headers are essential. Without them, Rapier3D physics fails silently and the chemistry grid cannot use shared memory.

### API Layer (Vercel Serverless Functions)

The api/ directory deploys automatically as serverless functions on Node.js with auto-parsed JSON bodies.

### Game Server (Railway)

Always-on Node.js process:
- Starts at boot, loads settings from database, begins ticking the world clock
- Accepts WebSocket connections on configurable port (default 8080)
- Broadcasts WORLD_SNAPSHOT at 10 Hz and BATCH_UPDATE messages as needed
- Persists sim time and time scale to database every 30 seconds
- Restarts automatically via Railway process supervision
- Note: The Railway server was found to be DOWN during a playtest session on 2026-03-24 (B-07); all multiplayer features were non-functional until the server was redeployed

### Science Companion Site (Vercel)

A separate Vercel project at https://universe-companion.vercel.app. Includes:
- AI science explainer for game mechanics
- FirstContactBanner (M13)
- Universe Map page at /universes (M15)

### Environment Variables Required

| Variable | Where Used | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| DATABASE_URL | API routes + Railway server | Neon Postgres connection string |
| CLERK_SECRET_KEY | API routes | Verifies Clerk JWTs server-side |
| ADMIN_USER_ID | API routes | Admin account Clerk user ID |
| VITE_ADMIN_USER_ID | Frontend build | Shows/hides admin button client-side |
| VITE_WS_URL | Frontend build | WebSocket URL for Railway server |

---

## 15. Status Website — Mission Control Dashboard

A live, public-facing dashboard at https://universe-sim-beryl.vercel.app/status/ — no login required. It shows where players are, what NPCs are doing, what era the simulation is in, and whether the server is running. Updates live at 10 Hz from the same Railway WebSocket server that powers the game.

### Layout

```
+----------------------------------------------------------+
|  UNIVERSE SIM  MISSION CONTROL  |  STELLAR ERA  |  SYNC  |  <- EpochBar
+------------------------------------------+---------------+
|                                          |               |
|                                          |  SERVER       |
|           SATELLITE MAP                  |  STATUS       |
|         (canvas, live positions)         |  SIDEBAR      |
|                                          |               |
+------------------------------------------+---------------+
|  ACTIVE AGENTS  [count]                                  |  <- PlayerRoster
|  [card] [card] [card] ...                                |
+----------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Components

**EpochBar** — Top strip showing app identity, current cosmological epoch (flashes gold on era transition), world time, time scale, PAUSED/LIVE badge, and SYNC/OFFLINE indicator.

**SatelliteMap** — Main panel. An HTML canvas (800x600 internal resolution) with:
- Procedural terrain generated with fractional Brownian motion once at load
- NPC dots color-coded by behavior state (green=wander, amber=gather, bright green=eat, blue=rest, purple=socialize)
- Player dots with pulsing rings and a color-coded health arc (green above 50%, orange 25-50%, red below 25%)
- Username labels above each player
- Coordinate grid, N/S/E/W compass, central crosshair, blinking LIVE indicator
- Click on a player dot to open their detail card

**ServerStats** — Right sidebar: connection status, players online count, NPCs active, NPC behavior breakdown bar chart, plain-English epoch description.

**PlayerRoster** — Bottom strip: scrollable player cards showing initials avatar, username, health bar, and coordinates. Clickable to open detail.

**PlayerDetail** — Slide-up modal on player click: initials avatar, username, Agent ID, health bar (glows red below 25%), X/Y/Z coordinates.

**Design aesthetic**: Near-black background (#060810), animated scrolling starfield, cyan accents (#00d4ff), IBM Plex Mono for data readouts, Orbitron for headings. Military mission control visual language.

## 17. Known Issues and Gaps

### Active Bugs

| ID | Issue | Severity | Impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| B-01 | admin.ts uses Web API style (Request/Response) and returns 500 errors in production | Medium | Admin player list fails to load. Admin panel opens but shows no players. |
| B-02 | No toast notification when auto-save fails | Low | Players have no feedback if save silently fails |
| B-03 | @clerk/clerk-react and @clerk/react both listed at different versions | Low | May cause import conflicts or bundle bloat |
| B-04 | Simulation grid state (fire, heat) is not persisted | Low | Active fires are lost on disconnect or page refresh |
| B-05 | Status site canvas is fixed at 800x600 | Low | Click detection may be slightly imprecise at extreme aspect ratios |
| B-07 | Railway WebSocket server intermittently DOWN | High | When down, all multiplayer features are non-functional: player visibility, fire sync, weather broadcast, WS-notified events. Requires Railway dashboard inspection and redeploy. |
| B-10 | Fire CRAFT recipe in the CRF panel produces an inert ITEM.FIRE item | Medium | The fire recipe (1x flint + 3x wood) outputs ITEM.FIRE. The real fire mechanic is separate: equip Flint and press Q near wood. The recipe is misleading. |
| B-17 | MAP coordinate display shows (0, 0) after bedroll respawn | Low | MAP panel shows incorrect position until the player starts moving. Workaround: start moving. |

## 18. Recommendations

### Critical (fix immediately)

1. **Fix admin.ts API handler style** — Rewrite to Node.js req/res style matching the other three routes. The admin player list is a production failure today.
2. **Investigate Railway WebSocket server stability** — B-07 showed the server was DOWN during a playtest session. Determine if this is a crash loop or a Railway instance issue; add a health check endpoint and alerting.
3. **Add WebSocket authentication for ADMIN_SET_TIME** — Any player can currently change the world clock. Add a server-side admin check.

### High Priority

4. **Wire fire extinguishing to chemistry grid** — WeatherRenderer sets isRain but the chemistry worker does not yet extinguish cells. This is M8 Track 1's remaining work.
5. **Complete steel age chain** — Fe + C carburization and quenching mechanic to unlock the next metal tier.
6. **Add save-failure toast notification** — Players need feedback when auto-save fails.
7. **Add settlement_stockpiles, discoveries, and planets tables to init-db** — All three M10/M13 tables require manual SQL creation on fresh deployments. Add CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements for all three.

### Medium Priority

8. **Sync watercraft position over WebSocket** — Sailing boats are not broadcast to other players.
9. **Move Velar cipher validation to server-side** — Add a /api/decode-velar route that checks the 8-symbol answer server-side and issues VELAR_DECODED from there.
10. **Persist nuclear radiation zone to database** — Store the active zone in a new world_hazards table so it survives page refresh.
11. **Add in-world capsule flight VFX** — A simple arc or particle trail from launch pad to orbit would make probe launches visible to other players.
12. **Remove duplicate @clerk/clerk-react dependency** — Remove version 5 from package.json; use only @clerk/react version 6.
13. **Investigate starvation damage threshold** — Playtest cycle 10 showed no health damage at hunger=22 after 26 minutes. Confirm whether the damage triggers at 0 exactly or whether it is broken at low-but-nonzero thresholds.
14. **Migrate player_saves.discoveries column to JSONB** — Change from TEXT to a native Postgres JSONB column so it can be indexed and queried.
15. **Implement artillery shell delivery system** — ITEM 65 (Artillery Shell) is an inert collectible. A cannon or siege engine would complete the loop.

### Low Priority / Long-term

16. **Add level-of-detail for terrain and buildings** — Animals have three LOD tiers; terrain and placed structures render at full detail at all distances.
17. **Persist simulation grid state** — Save fire and heat state to the database so it survives disconnection.
18. **Protect or remove /api/init-db** — Move behind admin auth or delete after initial setup.
19. **Add test coverage for M10-M14 systems** — Season timing math, price formula, Kepler orbit calculations, and reactor temperature physics are all strong candidates for deterministic unit tests.
20. **Add fishing rod cast animation** — The rod is visually static during the wait period.
21. **Add wolf pelt crafting recipe** — Wolf pelts are collected but unused.

## 19. Summary Scorecard

| Area | Rating | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Core Survival Loop | Excellent | All 7 slices verified end-to-end; freshwater from rivers; eating confirmed as forage/hunt-based |
| Chemistry and Physics | Excellent | Real Arrhenius combustion, reduction chemistry, gunpowder chemistry (M11), Rapier3D physics, river current push |
| Rivers | Excellent | Flow-field erosion, real valley geometry, Fresnel water, KCC push, drinkable freshwater (M9) |
| Seasons | Excellent | 40-min year, 4 seasons, terrain overlays, winter metabolic penalty, HUD widget (M10) |
| Sailing and Fishing | Good | Wind-driven raft and sailing boat with tacking, fishing rod, compass (M10) — watercraft not yet multiplayer-synced |
| Trade Economy | Good | Live supply/demand, settlement specializations, NPC caravans, ShopHUD, copper coins (M10) — stockpile table migration is manual |
| Animal AI | Good | Three species with distinct behavioral profiles; InstancedMesh rendering; ecosystem caps (M9) |
| Multiplayer | Needs Improvement | Server-authoritative world sync works but Railway WebSocket server went DOWN in testing (B-07); sailing still not synced |
| NPC Civilization | Good | 5 settlements, mayor NPCs, diplomatic envoys, war/peace declarations (M11), live economy (M10) |
| PvP and Outlaw System | Good | murder_count in DB, bounties, redemption quests, criminal alignment affected by diplomacy (M11) |
| Visual Quality | Excellent | Photorealism pass, day/night, weather, Fresnel rivers, seasonal overlays, real star constellations, rocket VFX, electric settlement lights, transit cinematic (M14) |
| Performance | Good | Workers offloaded, instanced rendering, LOD on animals, NodeHealthBar pool, shader warmup; ElectricLightPass 40 PointLights needs benchmarking at scale |
| Security | Needs Improvement | Admin WebSocket unauthenticated, admin.ts broken in production, Velar cipher client-side |
| Code Quality | Good | Clean structure, well-commented, removeItemForce + canAddItem safeguards added; test coverage only for 3 sim modules |
| Database and Data | Good | NPC memory, criminal records, world settings, settlement stockpiles, discoveries, planets, universes all persisted |
| Accessibility | Needs Improvement | Screen reader labels present but no keyboard-only play mode |
| Panel Completeness | Good | 7 of 8 sidebar panels have real content; Settings panel is the only remaining placeholder |
| Companion Site | Good | Live at https://universe-companion.vercel.app with science explainer, FirstContactBanner, and Universe Map (M15) |
| Gunpowder and Firearms | Good | Real KNO3+C+S Arrhenius reaction, musket as first chemical firearm (M11) |
| Castle and Fortification | Good | Multi-block wall/tower/gate, CastleRenderer, siege mechanics, civLevel 4-5 unlock (M11) |
| Astronomy | Good | Real constellation positions, telescope, moon phase, eclipse timing (M11) |
| Diplomacy | Good | Mayor NPCs, diplomatic envoys, war/peace declarations affecting criminal alignment (M11) |
| Space Age / Rocketry | Good | Countdown/ignition/ascent state machine, 3-layer exhaust VFX, screen shake (M12) |
| Radio | Good | Broadcast queue, tower registry, teal EM pulse rings (M12) |
| First Contact / Velar Signal | Good | 8-symbol Morse decoder, 12s cinematic, VELAR_DECODED broadcast, companion site banner (M13) — cipher validation should move server-side |
| Orbital Mechanics | Good | 3 Kepler planets, SVG solar map, Orbital Capsule, PROBE_LANDED, planet data in DB (M13) |
| Nuclear Reactor | Good | Fully operational 100kW reactor, meltdown at 800C, radiation zone, cleanup mission, ReactorWidget (M13) — radiation zone not yet persisted |
| Interplanetary Transit | Good | F-key transit, 20s cinematic with 300 streaming stars, procedural destination planet (M14) |
| Velar Language System | Good | Procedural 5-glyph SVG alien alphabet, decoding puzzle, gateway renderer (M14) |
| Universe Registry | Good | Neon DB universes table, multi-universe routing, gateway activation spawns new universe (M14) |
| Universe Map (Companion) | Good | SVG starfield map, civilization progress panels, 30s polling (M15) |
| M8 Remaining | In Progress | Fire extinguishing during rain and steel age still pending |
| **Overall Health** | **Good** | **The game now spans Stone Age through interplanetary travel and alien language contact. Playtesting sessions 1-12 found and fixed 9 bugs. Core survival loop is production-ready. Primary remaining gaps: Railway WebSocket stability (B-07), admin.ts in production (B-01), watercraft multiplayer sync, and several DB migration automation items.** |
